name: Motherwort
summary: Motherwort is an aerial-parts monograph with historical use around calm, palpitations, and menstrual-support positioning. It is useful for the system because it combines calming relevance with cardiovascular and uterine caution logic.
description: Motherwort is used in teas, tinctures, and capsules. Reported constituents include leonurine and stachydrine along with flavonoid fractions. Position cautiously: calming and cycle-support language can coexist with stronger contraindication gating for pregnancy and cardiovascular-medication overlap.
mechanism: Proposed mechanism includes mild GABAergic and autonomic-calming effects with additional cardiometabolic and smooth-muscle-adjacent actions attributed to leonurine and related alkaloids.
safetyNotes: May cause GI upset, sedation, or dizziness in some users; avoid framing as universally gentle because uterine and cardiovascular considerations matter.
interactions: and other agents affecting uterine tone or bleeding.
region: Native to Eurasia; naturalized widely in North America.
